t_ch: 去隔壁包厢给陌生人上压力…
t_en: Go putting pressure on strangers in the next room...

t_ch: 不愧是你，在无数的道路前，选择了可能是最为艰辛的一条道路。推开门，让他们知道，你来了。
t_en: As expected of you, among countless paths, you choose what might be the most challenging one. Push open that door and let them know: you've arrived.

t_ch: 很显然，他们对你的到来并没有任何预期…你是服务生，还是，一般路过的帮派成员？
t_en: Obviously, they aren't expecting you at all... Are you a waiter, or just some random gang member passing by?

t_ch: 他们在你的眼中看到了，啊！那是…卡拉OK之旅的休止符。
t_en: They see it in your eyes: Ah! That is... the end of their karaoke journey.

t_ch: 无言的压力让他们坐立不安，唱歌也拘谨了很多…他们想问你点什么，但是终究还是太怯懦了。
t_en: The unspoken pressure makes them fidget restlessly, and their singing becomes much more reserved... They want to ask you something, but in the end, they're just choked by fear.

t_ch: 毕竟，想杀人的眼神是藏不住的。
t_en: After all, if looks could kill, this one of yours would.

t_ch: …最后，他们主动更换了包厢，大堂经理面色铁青，向受到影响的双方都道了歉。
t_en: ...In the end, they voluntarily change their room, and the lobby manager, with a livid face, apologizes to both affected parties.

t_ch: 在冷暴力的对峙过程中，他们始终没有选择对抗，是因为他们忘不了你那一双凶手的眼睛。
t_en: Throughout the whole silent confrontation, they never choose to face you because they can't shake those murderous eyes of yours.

t_ch: 吾心吾行澄如鸽川，所作所为皆为欢愉——大概说的就是现在了。
t_en: "My heart and actions are as clear as Dovebrook. They are all those of 'Elation'." Now this must be that feeling.

t_ch: 您好，无梦咖啡很高兴为您服务。
t_en: Thank you for choosing Dreamless Coffee, where we're sleepy I mean happy to serve. What can we get started for ya?

t_ch: 鉴于您是到本店的第<unbreak>10000</unbreak>名顾客，这里将赠送您终身畅饮的资格！
t_en: As our <unbreak>10,000</unbreak>th customer, we're giving you a lifetime of free drinks on the house!

t_ch: 太好了，我真幸运。
t_en: Wow, I'm so lucky!

t_ch: 不会是骗人的吧？
t_en: You're not pulling my leg, are you?

t_ch: 请您放心，这是货真价实的。不过，本活动的最终解释权归本店所有。
t_en: Rest assured, this is <unbreak>100%</unbreak> genuine. However, we reserve the right of final interpretation for this promotion.

t_ch: …我其实只点一杯。
t_en: ...I actually only ordered one drink.

t_ch: 您要是拥有了终身畅饮的资格，您想喝一杯就喝一杯，想两杯就喝两杯。
t_en: If you had a lifetime free drink pass, you could have one drink when you want one, or two when you want two.

t_ch: 不过呢，我们需要收取您一些信用点用于激活会员资格。
t_en: However, we'll need to charge you some credits to activate your membership.

t_ch: 小意思，会员贵吗？
t_en: No biggie, is it expensive?

t_ch: 果然还是在骗人嘛！
t_en: I knew you were lying!

t_ch: 我们只收一杯饮品的价格，非常划算。但是，钱我们必须得收，毕竟我们也要做账嘛。
t_en: We only charge the price of one drink. It's a real bargain, but we do need to collect the money. After all, we've got to keep the books balanced.

t_ch: 升级会员的钱，我来出。
t_en: I'll cover the membership upgrade fee.

t_ch: 太好了，您付吧。
t_en: Great! You can pay for it.

t_ch: 一杯的饮料付一杯的钱…
t_en: A cup of money for a cup of drink...

t_ch: 没问题，您付吧。
t_en: No problem, you can pay for it.

t_ch: 不好意思，我不感兴趣。
t_en: Sorry, but I'm not interested.

t_ch: 没关系，我们会为您保留终身畅饮的资格，欢迎您随时回来。
t_en: No worries, we will save your lifetime free drink pass for you. Feel free to come back anytime.

t_ch: 已为您激活了终身畅饮的资格，希望您终身支持我们无梦咖啡。
t_en: Your lifetime free drink pass has been activated. We hope you'll continue to support Dreamless Coffee forever.

t_ch: 请问您现在点单吗？
t_en: Are you ready to order?

t_ch: 有没有什么推荐呢？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 不了，下次吧！
t_en: Nah, maybe next time!

t_ch: 没关系，欢迎您随时再来！
t_en: No worries, feel free to come back anytime!

t_ch: 您好，尊贵的本店第<unbreak>10000</unbreak>名顾客，无梦咖啡很期望您*购入*您终身畅饮的资格！
t_en: Hello, esteemed 10,000th customer of our establishment! Dreamless Coffee is thrilled to invite you to purchase your lifetime drinking privileges!

t_ch: 升级会员的钱，我来出。
t_en: I'll cover the membership upgrade fee.

t_ch: 太好了，呜呜呜～
t_en: That's awesome! *crying*

t_ch: 一杯的饮料付一杯的钱…
t_en: A cup of money for a cup of drink...

t_ch: 你放心，确实是一杯饮料钱。
t_en: Don't worry, it really only costs as much as a drink.

t_ch: 不好意思，我不感兴趣。
t_en: Sorry, but I'm not interested.

t_ch: 没关系，我们会为您保留终身畅饮的资格，欢迎您随时回来。
t_en: No worries, we will save your lifetime free drink pass for you. Feel free to come back anytime.

t_ch: 已为您激活了终身畅饮的资格，希望您终身支持我们无梦咖啡。
t_en: Your lifetime free drink pass has been activated. We hope you'll continue to support Dreamless Coffee forever.

t_ch: 请问您现在点单吗？
t_en: Are you ready to order?

t_ch: 有没有什么推荐呢？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 不了，下次吧！
t_en: Nah, maybe next time!

t_ch: 没关系，欢迎您随时再来！
t_en: No worries, feel free to come back anytime!

t_ch: 您好，无梦咖啡很高兴为您服务。请问，您想喝点什么？
t_en: Hello, welcome to Dreamless Coffee. We're happy to serve you. What would you like to drink?

t_ch: 让我想想点杯什么…
t_en: Let me think about what to order...

t_ch: 可以随便聊聊么？
t_en: Can we chat for a bit?

t_ch: 现在没有其他的客人，可以简单聊聊。
t_en: There are no other customers right now, so we can have a casual chat.

t_ch: 这里只能堂食不能打包吗？
t_en: Is it dine-in only? No takeout?

t_ch: 是的，我们饮品的最佳风味品质的期限很短的，建议是立刻饮用呢。
t_en: Yes, our drinks have a very short optimal consumption window, so we recommend drinking them right away.

t_ch: 感觉到处都是你们连锁店…
t_en: It feels like you have chain stores everywhere...

t_ch: 您的感觉没有错，我们在二维市有很多店面。
t_en: You're right, we have many stores in Duomension City.

t_ch: 我们的目标是——只能说您不想喝，决不能说您喝不到。
t_en: Our goal is: you can say you don't want it, but you can never say you can't get it.

t_ch: 不同店面会有什么区别吗？
t_en: What's the difference between the stores?

t_ch: 当然，现在的差别还不太大…但在如今追求个性的时代里，无梦咖啡也即将推出「主理人」模式！
t_en: Well, there isn't that much of a difference... but in today's era of pursuing individuality, Dreamless Coffee is about to take a "managerial" approach!

t_ch: 到时候，每一家都有自己的口味，每一家都有自己的规矩，每一家都有自己的熟客。
t_en: By then, every shop will have its own signature flavor, its own rules, and its own regulars.

t_ch: 「中杯、大杯、超大杯」也会进化成「大杯，超大杯，超级大大杯」！
t_en: "Medium, large, and extra large" will evolve into "large, extra large, and super mega large"!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 不好意思，今天就算了。
t_en: Sorry, I'll pass for today.

t_ch: 没问题，欢迎您再来！
t_en: No problem, come back anytime!

t_ch: 这是今日推荐的茶饮～
t_en: Here's today's recommended drink~

t_ch: 「金光骤现」芒果思慕雪
t_en: "Golden Flash" Mango Smoothie

t_ch: 淡黄果浆微微晃动，芒果的甜香沉浸在冰沙与酸奶之中，一丝丝凉意裹着果肉滑落到喉间。
t_en: The pale yellow fruit purée swirls gently. The sweet fragrance of mango is infused within the smoothie and yogurt, while bits of fruit slide down your throat with a cool, refreshing sensation.

t_ch: 「丝滑操作」抹茶椰奶冰沙
t_en: "Smooth Operator" Matcha Coconut Milk Smoothie

t_ch: 抹茶绿混着椰奶白，冰沙沁凉，微涩的茶香和清甜的椰香在嘴里一气化开…实在是恰到好处的味道～
t_en: The green of matcha mixes with the white coconut milk. The resulting smoothie is delightfully chilled, and the subtle astringent of tea mingles perfectly with the fragrant sweetness of coconut... A perfect blend!

t_ch: 「处处惊喜」蕉蕉芭菲
t_en: "Delightful Surprise" Banana Parfait

t_ch: 软糯丸子放于雪顶之上，巧克力饼干镶嵌在绵密的奶油层之中，细腻绵密的蕉蕉垫底，一口满足。
t_en: Soft rice ball rests atop a snowy peak where chocolate cookies lie nestled in a layer of velvety cream with a smooth and creamy banana base. It's pure bliss in every bite.

t_ch: 「开始幻想」黄桃冰淇淋漂浮
t_en: "Fantasy's Beginnings" Yellow Peach Ice Cream Float

t_ch: 冰淇淋奶油浮在黄桃味汽水上，就像是云朵一样载沉载浮，吸管搅起蜜香漩涡，漾开甜蜜的沙沙声。
t_en: The ice cream drifts atop the peach soda like a cloud, bobbing up and down. As the straw stirs up a honey-scented whirlpool, it creates a sweet fizzing melody.

t_ch: 「极致输出」爆打青柠茶
t_en: "Maximum DMG" Hand-Crushed Lime Tea

t_ch: 被暴打一顿的青柠片在杯里翻滚。酸冽冰浪裹茶汤撞进杯底，猛吸一口，清新的凉气直冲天灵盖！
t_en: Battered lime slices tumble in the glass. The sharp, icy waves of citrus-infused tea crash to the bottom... One big sip, and BAM! That crisp, cool sensation shoots straight to the crown of your head!

t_ch: 您好，请问您有预约吗？我们这里是会员制牙科诊所。
t_en: Hello, do you have an appointment? This is a members-only dental clinic.

t_ch: 我想办理这里的会员！
t_en: I want to sign up for a membership here!

t_ch: 当然，请在这里登记下个人信息，并支付诊所的会员费用。
t_en: Of course, please fill in your personal information here and pay the clinic's membership fee.

t_ch: 恭喜您{F#女士}{M#先生}，您已经成为了「美牙牙科」的年费会员，您将在这里得到二相乐园最好的牙齿护理服务。
t_en: Congratulations {F#Madam}{M#Sir}, you are now a premium member of Beauteeth Dentist. You will receive the best dental care services in all of Planarcadia.

t_ch: 值班的医师正好有空的档期，请问您现在需要护理服务吗？
t_en: The doctor on duty has a free slot right now, do you require medical care?

t_ch: 当然，麻烦您了。
t_en: Of course, thank you for your help.

t_ch: 请稍等，医师需要准备时间，在等候期间，麻烦您勾选一下需要的服务。
t_en: Please wait while the doctor prepares. In the meantime, kindly select the services you need.

t_ch: 抱歉，下次再来。
t_en: Sorry, perhaps next time.

t_ch: 好的，请让我们下次再来服务尊贵的您。
t_en: Thank you, we look forward to serving you, esteemed guest.

t_ch: 原来如此，下次再来。
t_en: I see. Till next time, then.

t_ch: 没问题，如果有需要随时来咨询。
t_en: No problem, feel free to come to me for advice anytime.

t_ch: 您好，尊敬的美牙铂金会员用户{NICKNAME}，欢迎光临美牙牙科。
t_en: Hello, esteemed Beauteeth Platinum member {NICKNAME}, welcome to Beauteeth Dentist.

t_ch: 我需要牙齿护理的服务。
t_en: I need dental care services.

t_ch: 想要咨询一下这的服务。
t_en: I'd like to inquire about your services here.

t_ch: 当然了，有什么想要咨询的，我会尽量回答。
t_en: Of course, I'll do my best to answer any questions you may have.

t_ch: 感觉这里的牙科诊所还挺多。
t_en: Looks like there are quite a few dental clinics around here.

t_ch: 您的观察很仔细。这可能要归功于首席执行官真珠女士。自从她就任以来，牙科治疗归属于医疗保险范围内，居民们个人承担的费用很少。
t_en: You're very observant. This is probably thanks to the CEO, Madam Pearl. Since she took office, dental care has been covered under medical insurance, and residents only have to pay a small portion of the costs themselves.

t_ch: 另外，二相乐园也提供专属于幻造种的牙科服务，这是需要注入愿力的技术才能做到的特殊服务——在银河别处找不到第二处了。
t_en: Also, Planarcadia provides specialized dental services for the imagenae. It's a unique service that requires Wishpower-infused technology. You won't find anything like it anywhere else in the cosmos!

t_ch: 说是牙齿护理，其实是刷牙？
t_en: So this "dental care" thing is just a fancier way of brushing your teeth?

t_ch: 客人可不要小看本诊所的刷牙服务哦。
t_en: Don't underestimate our clinic's teeth cleaning service.

t_ch: 您自己刷牙的时候，用的是牙膏不过是——负责摩擦的水合硅石，产生泡沫的月桂醇硫酸酯钠，以及防蛀的氟化物之类的，对吧？
t_en: When you brush your teeth, your toothpaste is just made of hydrated silica for scrubbing, sodium lauryl sulfate for foam, and fluoride compounds for cavity prevention, right?

t_ch: 你在说些什么呢？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 难道说你们不是？
t_en: And your services are not the same?

t_ch: 我们不是。
t_en: They're not.

t_ch: 您是否常常听人用「武装到牙齿」来形容一个人装备极其精良，做好了最充分的准备，全副武装到了极点。
t_en: You've probably heard the phrase "armed to the teeth" used to describe someone who's extremely well-equipped, thoroughly prepared, and geared up to the max.

t_ch: 毕竟牙齿是人身体上最后、也是最不可能被武装的部位。如果连牙齿都武装上了，那就意味着没有任何一个细节被遗漏！但是，谁能做到呢？
t_en: After all, teeth are the last and most unlikely body part to be weaponized. If even your teeth are armed, it means not a single detail was overlooked! But who could possibly pull that off?

t_ch: 美牙牙科是能做到的。
t_en: Beauteeth Dentist can do it.

t_ch: 我们诊所购入的牙膏用的都是银河中至臻至纯的纯粹元素——只有接受了我们的护理服务，您才能够告诉别人，您的确做到了「武装到牙齿」。
t_en: Our clinic's toothpaste is made with the purest, most pristine elements in the cosmos. Only after receiving our dental care can you truly say that you're "armed to the teeth"!

t_ch: 你们这里的服务安全吗？
t_en: Is that safe?

t_ch: 您更喜欢真心话还是大冒险？
t_en: Truth or dare?

t_ch: 真心话…
t_en: Truth...

t_ch: 我们当然是安全的啦，啊哈哈～
t_en: Of course we're safe, ahaha~

t_ch: 大冒险…
t_en: Dare...

t_ch: 您直接做一次就明白了。
t_en: Do it once and you'll get it.

t_ch: 什么情况…
t_en: What's with that...

t_ch: 我们总被问起这样的问题，就算我们说「非常安全」也会被当作是在开玩笑。
t_en: We're always getting asked these questions, and even when we say "it's perfectly safe," they think we're joking.

t_ch: 所以，我们的回答策略是主动出击！
t_en: So, our response strategy is to be proactive!

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 这次就不用了，下次吧。
t_en: Not this time. Maybe next time.

t_ch: 期待您的下次光临。
t_en: We look forward to your next visit.

t_ch: 请问您需要怎样的服务？
t_en: How may I help you?

t_ch: 「终极刷牙套餐」
t_en: Ultimate Dental Care Package

t_ch: 本店采用全银河最先进的刷牙技术，配合至臻至纯的元素营养牙膏，确保您独一无二的体验。
t_en: Our establishment uses the most advanced tooth-brushing technology in the entire cosmos, paired with the purest elemental nutrient toothpaste, guaranteeing you a truly one-of-a-kind experience.

t_ch: 犟人•刷牙套餐
t_en: Stubborn Man's Dental Care Package

t_ch: 这是最本源、最硬核的刷牙方式，我们只提供唯一的一种牙膏。
t_en: This is the most fundamental and hardcore way to brush your teeth. We offer only one type of toothpaste.

t_ch: 物理•牙膏
t_en: Physical Toothpaste

t_ch: 所谓「犟人精神」就是绝不改变自己，坚持到底，以极致的态度对待产品和服务的职业精神。
t_en: The "Stubborn Man's Spirit" is a professional ethos that means never compromising one's core self, persevering to the very end, and approaching products and services with the utmost dedication.

t_ch: 专注于刷牙领域，便是专注于「刷」这一道工序——犟人必须有一生只做一件事的执着。
t_en: By focusing on the field of brushing teeth, one focuses on the single act of "brushing." A stubborn man must have the dedication to do just one thing for life.

t_ch: 你明白了吗？对于犟人来说，刷牙与打磨精致的雕塑是一样的。
t_en: You understand, don't you? For a stubborn man, brushing teeth is no different from polishing a delicate sculpture.

t_ch: 或许…真正的刷牙，不需要任何复杂的科技，不需要任何化合物——究极的「物理刷牙」，仅凭摩擦便能征服所有龋病、牙周病…甚至是牙髓炎、根尖周炎。
t_en: Perhaps... true brushing doesn't require any complex technology or chemical compounds. The ultimate "physical brushing" can conquer all cavities, periodontal disease... and even pulpitis and periapical periodontitis through friction alone.

t_ch: 只有用最物理的方式，你才能感受到——所谓的水合硅石，所谓的「泡沫」，所谓的氟化物终究只是技巧，在终极的暴力面前，技巧的纯度还是太低了。
t_en: Only through the most physical means can you truly feel it. All that talk of hydrated silica, so-called "foam," and fluorides are just techniques. When faced with ultimate violence, the effectiveness of such techniques falls way too short.

t_ch: 时尚•刷牙套餐
t_en: Trendy Dental Care Package

t_ch: 这是最新潮、最尖端的刷牙方式，我们提供了四种不同的牙膏。
t_en: This is the trendiest, most cutting-edge way to brush your teeth. We offer four different types of toothpaste.

t_ch: 风•牙膏
t_en: Wind Toothpaste

t_ch: 你见过在飓风中报道的电视记者吗？他们在狂风中甚至无法闭上两片嘴唇。
t_en: You know those TV reporters who cover hurricane news? They can't even close their lips in those crazy winds.

t_ch: 换句话说，一切的牙齿污垢将在真正的风暴面前无所遁形。
t_en: In other words, all that dental plaque will have nowhere to hide once the real storm hits.

t_ch: 数千万的微型龙卷风在齿缝间螺旋穿梭，你第一次感觉到「通透的感觉」。原来，你的牙齿曾经背负着如此沉重的负担，幸好，现在它们已经解脱了！
t_en: Millions of tiny tornadoes spiral through the gaps between your teeth, and for the first time, you experience that "thoroughly clean feeling." Who knew your teeth were carrying such a heavy burden all this time? Thank goodness they're finally free!

t_ch: 但是你要千万小心，风•牙膏也是有副作用的——
t_en: But be extremely careful, Wind Toothpaste has side effects too.

t_ch: 刚刷完牙不久，你说出的话都带着风…原本十秒能讲完的事，你只消一秒就能讲完，别人可能根本听不清楚你说了什么，还怕你咬到了舌头。
t_en: Right after brushing your teeth, your words will come out all breathy... You will blurt out things that would normally take ten seconds to say in one. People will probably be unable to even make out what you're saying, and worry that you might bite your tongue.

t_ch: 火•牙膏
t_en: Fire Toothpaste

t_ch: 火•牙膏的本质是践行「火焰即净化」的法则——
t_en: Fire toothpaste embodies the law of "purification by fire."

t_ch: 它不对抗污垢，而是将污垢转化为光与热。
t_en: It doesn't fight against filth, but converts it into light and heat.

t_ch: 虽然这么打比方可能有些令人不适，但是你听说过「垃圾焚烧」吗？
t_en: This might be an uncomfortable comparison, but have you heard of "trash incineration"?

t_ch: 垃圾焚烧是一种通过高温对垃圾进行热化学处理的过程，不仅能够减少垃圾的体积，还能在燃烧的热能转化发电与供热，是「变废为宝」的极致工程。
t_en: Trash incineration is a thermochemical treatment process that uses high temperatures to process waste. Not only does it reduce the volume of trash, but it also converts combustion heat into power generation and heating, making it the ultimate engineering feat of "turning waste into treasure."

t_ch: 那些牙周污垢，已经在刷牙的过程中转变成了你的生命能量——这就是火焰的意义。
t_en: That periodontal plaque has already been converted into your life energy during the brushing process. That's the meaning of fire.

t_ch: 冰•牙膏
t_en: Ice Toothpaste

t_ch: 无数牙膏都尝试着接近「冰」能达到的体验——它们所产生的清凉，终究只是拙劣的模仿。
t_en: Countless toothpastes have tried to replicate the sensation that "ice" delivers, but their cooling effect is nothing more than a poor imitation.

t_ch: 使用冰•牙膏本身就是一种禁忌，因为它的清洁过程终究是更加接近「魔法」一些。
t_en: Using Ice Toothpaste itself is taboo, since its cleaning process is ultimately closer to "magic" than anything else.

t_ch: 冰•牙膏反应了一种来自思维层面的「秩序哲学」…它不需要抗污垢，它统治污垢——在冰雪的王国里，污垢天然是混乱与失序，会被排斥与剖离。
t_en: Ice toothpaste embodies the "philosophy of order" on a cognitive level... It doesn't need to fight against filth, it rules over filth. In the kingdom of ice and snow, filth is inherently chaos and disorder, destined to be rejected and severed.

t_ch: 每一次刷牙都是在对抗熵增，每一次摩擦都是在凝固微观世界——「绝对洁净即绝对静止」。
t_en: Every time you brush your teeth, you're fighting against entropy. Every friction is congealing the microscopic world: "Absolute cleanliness equals absolute stillness."

t_ch: 顺带一提，刷完牙之后，最好喝一些温暖的姜茶，千万别喝冷水，这是为了你好。
t_en: By the way, it's best to drink some warm ginger tea after brushing your teeth. Whatever you do, don't drink cold water. I'm telling you this for your own good.

t_ch: 雷•牙膏
t_en: Lightning Toothpaste

t_ch: 你口腔中酥麻的刺痛感，被控制在令人振奋而非痛苦的感官体验之内。
t_en: The tingling sensation in your mouth is kept at a level that's more exhilarating than painful.

t_ch: 它在不接触水的情况下，做到了清洁的效果——至纯至高的能量场，将牙菌斑、茶渍、咖啡渍等等污垢，分解为最基本的分子或者原子。
t_en: Without even touching water, it achieves a cleaning effect. The purest, most sublime energy field breaks down plaque, tea stains, coffee stains, and other grime into their most basic molecules or atoms.

t_ch: 因此，在不少牙膏领域的专家观点中，雷电是一种解构主义牙膏。
t_en: Therefore, according to many experts in the toothpaste field, lightning is a deconstructive toothpaste.

t_ch: 刚刷完牙不久，我们不建议您使用任何电子设备，也不建议立即喝水…最好是等待身体放电完成，否则后果自负。
t_en: Since you just brushed your teeth, we advise against using any electronic devices or drinking water right away... It would be better to wait for your body to finish discharging the electricity. Otherwise, you'll have to deal with the consequences yourself.

t_ch: 曾经有流行歌手在登台演出前，使用了雷•牙膏刷牙，确保自己能够轻松唱出电音…这也算是一种小巧思了。
t_en: There was once a pop singer who used Lightning Toothpaste before going on stage to ensure they could effortlessly sing to electronica music... That's quite a clever little hack.

t_ch: 光影•刷牙套餐
t_en: Light & Shadow Dental Care Package

t_ch: 这是最创新、最具实验精神的刷牙方式，我们提供了两种不同的牙膏。
t_en: This is the most innovative and experimental way to brush your teeth. We've got two different kinds of toothpaste for you.

t_ch: 量子•牙膏
t_en: Quantum Toothpaste

t_ch: 如果你同样认为「换新」比「维修」更彻底，那么你确实更适合量子•牙膏。
t_en: If you also believe that "replacing" is more thorough than "repairing," then you're definitely better suited for Quantum Toothpaste.

t_ch: 每次刷牙，都会随机连接平行宇宙的牙齿状态——换句话说，刷牙并非是「清洁」的行为，而是筛选多重现实的可能性。
t_en: Every time you brush your teeth, you randomly connect to the dental state of a parallel universe. In other words, brushing your teeth isn't an act of "cleaning," but rather, filtering through the possibilities of multiple realities.

t_ch: 即便你的牙齿受到了「致命的伤害」，也能用另一个「完好无损」的牙齿来替代它。你的牙齿将获得无限次的「复活」，任何污垢都在平行宇宙前变得毫无意义。
t_en: Even if your teeth suffer "fatal damage," you can replace them with other "perfectly intact" teeth. Your teeth will gain infinite "resurrections," and any filth becomes meaningless in the face of the parallel universe.

t_ch: 但是，你必须千万小心，同一颗牙如果在同一世界中相遇，它们会发生剧烈的排斥反应，相互挤压直至最终毁灭…这也是「廉价把戏」的代价之一。
t_en: However, you must be extremely careful. If the same tooth encounters itself in the same world, they will trigger a violent rejection reaction, crushing each other until their ultimate destruction... This is one of the costs of using a cheap trick.

t_ch: 虚数•牙膏
t_en: Imaginary Toothpaste

t_ch: 有牙膏领域的伟人曾说过「人类对虚数的开发不足1%。」
t_en: A great philosopher from the toothpaste industry once said, "Humanity has barely tapped into 1% of imaginary numbers."

t_ch: 如果说其他的牙膏仍在执着于「肮脏」与「洁净」的二分法时，虚数•牙膏则像是在抹平认知空间…「肮脏」与「洁净」都将从实数宇宙的牙齿上彻底消失。
t_en: While other toothpastes are still obsessed with the dichotomy of "dirty" and "clean," Imaginary Toothpaste seems to be smoothing out the cognitive space... Both "dirty" and "clean" will completely vanish from the teeth's real universe.

t_ch: 也许「旁白」的概念也应失效——虚数•牙膏的讲述者，终究只能是牙齿本身。
t_en: Perhaps even the concept of a "narrator" should be invalidated. After all, the true narrator of Imaginary Toothpaste can only be the teeth themselves.

t_ch: 当虚数•牙膏宣布「污垢不存在时」，与之相关的概念便被删除了。
t_en: When Imaginary Toothpaste declared "Filth does not exist," all related concepts were deleted.

t_ch: 不要去试着理解，朋友。去感受。
t_en: Don't try to understand it, friend. Feel it.

t_ch: 不存在的概念，解决了存在的问题…这便是虚数•牙膏。
t_en: A non-existent concept solving an existing problem... That's Imaginary Toothpaste for you.

t_ch: 「幻造洗牙套餐」
t_en: Imagenated Dental Cleaning Package

t_ch: 本店将为您的牙齿注入愿力，使之成为愿力充盈的幻造牙齿，您打算试试吗？
t_en: Our shop can inject Wishpower into your teeth, turning them into imagenated teeth filled with Wishpower. Would you like to give it a try?

t_ch: 「铂金焕彩计划」
t_en: Platinum Radiance Project

t_ch: 黄金，自古以来黄金就是硬通货，是财富最直接的象征。
t_en: Gold has been the universal currency since ancient times, and is the most direct symbol of wealth.

t_ch: 你「想要更加富有」的愿望注入到牙齿里，完成了对牙齿的幻造礼装——
t_en: Your wish "to become richer" was injected into your teeth, completing the Imagenated Mystic Code...

t_ch: 大金牙！
t_en: Big Gold Tooth!

t_ch: 对于一夜暴富的人来说，需要一种快速、外显的方式宣告自己的经济地位提升！
t_en: For those who get rich overnight, they need a quick and flashy way to announce their upgraded financial status!

t_ch: 这不过是社会的偏见。实际上黄金对牙龈非常友好，很少有过敏或者排异反应，不易磨损、崩裂，使用寿命极长，甚至可以用几十年。
t_en: That's actually just societal prejudice. In fact, gold is very gentle on the gums, rarely causes allergies or structural deviation, doesn't wear down easily or rupture, and has an extremely long lifespan that lasts for decades.

t_ch: 往坏了想，如果真有一天你不再继续开拓，流落异乡、山穷水尽之时，你会感谢自己曾有一颗金牙——至少，大金牙算是你的固定资产。
t_en: On the downside, if one day you stop trailblazing and find yourself stranded in a strange place and down on luck, you'll be thankful for that gold tooth. At least it's one asset you can count on.

t_ch: 「铂金焕彩计划」体验快结束了…反正都是幻造的，不如加把劲，弄一口大金牙，再印上「冲！冲！{NICKNAME}！」肯定很引人注目的。
t_en: The "Platinum Radiance Project" experience is almost over... Since it's all imagenated anyway, why not go all out? Get a huge gold tooth with "GO! GO! {NICKNAME}!" engraved on it. That'd definitely turn some heads!

t_ch: 「生命复苏计划」
t_en: "Life Rejuvenation Project"

t_ch: 生命力，自古以来就是有灵生物所求之物，即便是长生种也不例外。
t_en: Life force has long been sought after by all sentient beings since ancient times, and even the long-life species are no exception.

t_ch: 你「想要更加有生命力」的愿望注入到牙齿里，完成了对牙齿的幻造礼装——
t_en: Your wish "to be more full of life" was injected into your teeth, completing the Imagenated Mystic Code...

t_ch: 你的牙齿出现了生态系统！
t_en: There's a whole ecosystem growing in your teeth!

t_ch: 这对于「想要更加有生命力」的愿望来说，「牙齿变成了星球」还真是一个绝佳的实现方式！
t_en: For someone who "wanted to be more full of life," having "teeth turn into planets" is an excellent way to make it happen!

t_ch: 你看看你的牙齿，它已经产生了大陆板块，山川大海，各种各样的生物…那种勃勃生机、万物竞发的境界，犹在眼前。
t_en: Look at your teeth! They've developed tectonic plates, mountains, and oceans, all kinds of life forms... That vibrant ecosystem teeming with life, where everything's flourishing and thriving. It's like a whole world right before my eyes.

t_ch: …好像不太对啊，你的牙齿确实有生命力了，可是生命力…完全没有分给你。
t_en: ...Something's not right here. Your teeth definitely did get some life force, but that life force... wasn't shared with you at all.

t_ch: 「生命复苏计划」体验快结束了…反正都是幻造的，希望这段期间它们还没有实现载人航空吧。
t_en: The "Life Rejuvenation Project" experience is almost over... Since it's all imagenated anyway, I hope they haven't achieved manned flight during this period.

t_ch: 「纯美精神计划」
t_en: "Spirit of Beauty Project"

t_ch: 追求美，自古以来就是人们深层心理动机，而「明眸皓齿」是无可争议的主流审美标准。
t_en: The pursuit of beauty has been a deep psychological drive since ancient times, and "bright eyes and white teeth" has been an undisputed mainstream aesthetic standard.

t_ch: 你「想要更加美丽」的愿望注入到牙齿里，完成了对牙齿的幻造礼装——
t_en: Your wish "to become more beautiful" was injected into your teeth, completing the Imagenated Mystic Code...

t_ch: 你的牙齿走上了「纯美」的命途！
t_en: Your teeth have embarked on the Path of Beauty!

t_ch: 这绝非一颗凡俗的碳酸钙聚合物，这是被伊德莉拉亲吻过的钻石水晶！
t_en: This is no mere calcium carbonate aggregate, it's a diamond crystal kissed by Idrila THEMSELF!

t_ch: 它的形态，是天才俱乐部毕生追求的完美椭圆与黄金分割的终极结合！
t_en: Its form is the ultimate fusion of a perfect ellipse and the golden ratio that the Genius Society has pursued their entire lives!

t_ch: 它的釉质，比仙舟朱明御窑千挑万选的釉彩更加温润，比二相乐园的幻月更加皎洁！
t_en: Its glaze is more lustrous than the meticulously selected glazes of the Xianzhou Zhuming's imperial kiln, and more pristine than the Phantasmoon of Planarcadia!

t_ch: 您唇间展露的刹那，将是众生莫大的荣幸…公司应该给您的牙齿投保，那保额恐怕是天文数字……
t_en: That fleeting smile of yours would be the greatest honor for all living beings... The IPC should get insurance for those teeth, though the cost of the premium would probably be astronomical...

t_ch: 「纯美精神计划」体验快结束了…多照照镜子，再龇牙咧嘴地臭美一会吧。
t_en: The "Spirit of Beauty Project" experience is almost over... Go on, check yourself out in the mirror and smile smugly while you still can.

t_ch: 欢迎光临怒涛小酒馆。客人…看您的样子，难道说是外地来的游客？
t_en: Welcome to Roaring Tide Tavern. Say... by the looks of you, might you be a traveler from out of town?

t_ch: 什么意思，你们不接待游客？
t_en: What do you mean you don't accept visitors?

t_ch: 不不不，我们接待的，您放心，我们没有赶客的意思。
t_en: No, no, no, we'll take care of you, don't worry. We have no intention of turning you away.

t_ch: 外地游客？我是老二维市人。
t_en: Tourist? I'm a born and bred Duomension City local.

t_ch: 别开玩笑了，我一眼就看出了您不是二相乐园的人。
t_en: Cut the jokes. I can tell at a glance that you're not from Planarcadia.

t_ch: 是这样的，我们是二相乐园旅游发展委员会的指定合作商铺…这个我就不详细解释了，简而言之，外地的游客来我们店消费是有优惠的！
t_en: So here's the deal. We're a designated partner shop of the Planarcadia Tourism Development Committee... I won't get into the details, but long story short, out-of-town visitors get special discounts at our store!

t_ch: 仅限二相乐园的外地游客，可以购买本店的「二维市观光文旅消费券」。
t_en: Only visitors from outside Planarcadia can purchase our "Duomension City Cultural Tourism Vouchers."

t_ch: 「消费券」生效期间，在一定价格区间，您在本店产生的一切饮食消费都将由旅游发展委员会承担…所以客人希望现在就办理吗？
t_en: While the "Vouchers" are available, within a certain price range, all your food and beverage expenses at our establishment will be covered by the Tourism Development Board... So, would you like to process it now?

t_ch: 快办理观光消费消费券！
t_en: Get the Vouchers now!

t_ch: 不用了，我还是排队吧。
t_en: Nah, I'll just wait in line.

t_ch: 「二维市观光文旅消费券」可以免去排队叫号的等待，如果客人坚持排队的话，您的前面还有大约<unbreak>78</unbreak>桌客人，预计排队时间超过1天。
t_en: The "Duomension City Cultural Tourism Voucher" lets you skip the queue number system. If you insist on waiting in line, there are approximately <unbreak>78</unbreak> tables ahead of you, with an estimated waiting time of over 1 day.

t_ch: …你们是认真的吗？
t_en: ...Are you serious?

t_ch: 我们一直都是认真的。
t_en: We've always been serious.

t_ch: 我有别的事，先离开了。
t_en: I got to go now.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临。
t_en: Come back next time.

t_ch: 客人您的「消费券」已经生效了，里面的空桌正在收拾，客人请稍等一下。
t_en: Your "Voucher" is now active. We're cleaning up a table inside. Please wait a moment, dear guest.

t_ch: 欢迎光临怒涛小酒馆。里面的空桌正在收拾，客人请稍等一下。
t_en: Welcome to Roaring Tide Tavern. We're cleaning up an empty table inside. Please wait a moment, dear guest.

t_ch: 让我看看你们的菜单吧。
t_en: Let me see your menu.

t_ch: 当然，咱们店里别的不说，食材都是当季最新鲜的。您随便选，都好吃！
t_en: Of course, if nothing else, all our ingredients are the freshest of the season. Take your pick. Everything's delicious!

t_ch: 海原市鲑鱼片。
t_en: Seafeld City's Raw Salmon Slice.

t_ch: 那么，客人您里面请，我们很快就给您上齐。
t_en: Please come in, dear guest. We'll have your order ready in no time.

t_ch: 暖黄色灯光下杯盘参差，隔帘透出餐台雾气，木廊尽头飘来木弦琴的余音。
t_en: Under the warm yellow lights, dishes are scattered about, steam wafts through the curtain from the dining table, and the lingering notes of a wooden lute drift from the end of the wooden corridor.

t_ch: 海原市特产的鲑鱼片，橘红润泽，纹理细腻柔润。入口时，冰凉滑嫩，肥腴软糯，那是海洋特有的清甜…蘸上淡酱油与芥末，鲜味甘甜，如融化在舌尖的脂膏。
t_en: The raw salmon slice, a specialty of Seafeld City, boasts a lustrous orange hue and delicate, silky texture. As it melts in your mouth, it's cool and tender, with a rich, buttery softness and that distinctive oceanic sweetness... Dipped in light soy sauce and wasabi, its umami and sweet flavors dissolve like velvet on your tongue.

t_ch: 脆脆薯条。
t_en: Crispy Fries.

t_ch: 那么，客人您里面请，我们很快就给您上齐。
t_en: Please come in, dear guest. We'll have your order ready in no time.

t_ch: 暖黄色灯光下杯盘参差，隔帘透出餐台雾气，木廊尽头飘来木弦琴的余音。
t_en: Under the warm yellow lights, dishes are scattered about, steam wafts through the curtain from the dining table, and the lingering notes of a wooden lute drift from the end of the wooden corridor.

t_ch: 金色脆壳在齿间绽开——匹诺康尼风味的焦香蓬勃而出，马铃薯的甜糯在酥脆掩护下悄然蔓延。
t_en: The golden crust shatters between the teeth, releasing the toasted aroma unique to Penacony, while beneath the crispness, the sugary tenderness of the potato quietly unfolds.

t_ch: 招牌深夜泡面。
t_en: Signature Late-Night Instant Noodles.

t_ch: 那么，客人您里面请，我们很快就给您上齐。
t_en: Please come in, dear guest. We'll have your order ready in no time.

t_ch: 暖黄色灯光下杯盘参差，隔帘透出餐台雾气，木廊尽头飘来木弦琴的余音。
t_en: Under the warm yellow lights, dishes are scattered about, steam wafts through the curtain from the dining table, and the lingering notes of a wooden lute drift from the end of the wooden corridor.

t_ch: 招牌深夜泡面冒着热气。雪白的面条浸在琥珀色牛羊骨高汤之中，面条麦香清韧，溏心蛋温润，酱牛肉片肥瘦相宜…实在是好滋味。
t_en: Steam rises from the Signature Late-Night Instant Noodles. The pristine white noodles bathe in amber-colored broth of beef and lamb bones, with the noodles offering a perfect wheaty chewiness, the soft-boiled egg rich and creamy, and the braised beef striking just the right balance between leanness and fattiness... truly a delectable treat.

t_ch: 特色烤肉串。
t_en: House BBQ Skewers.

t_ch: 那么，客人您里面请，我们很快就给您上齐。
t_en: Please come in, dear guest. We'll have your order ready in no time.

t_ch: 暖黄色灯光下杯盘参差，隔帘透出餐台雾气，木廊尽头飘来木弦琴的余音。
t_en: Under the warm yellow lights, dishes are scattered about, steam wafts through the curtain from the dining table, and the lingering notes of a wooden lute drift from the end of the wooden corridor.

t_ch: 甄选山野走地鸡，搭配山中菌菇，带着炭火炙烤的气息，一串肥瘦油亮。焦香气息的油脂顺着串签流淌，在唇齿之间迸发出鲜嫩的肉汁…哦，你的美，是美拉德反应的美。
t_en: Carefully selected free-range chicken, paired with wild mushrooms from the mountains, grilled with a charred aroma, each skewer glistening with the perfect balance of lean and fatty meat. The fragrant juices drip along the skewer, bursting with tender meat juices between your lips and teeth... oh, the beauty is all about the Maillard reaction.

t_ch: 全部都来一份。
t_en: I'll take one of everything.

t_ch: 那么，客人您里面请，我们很快就给您上齐。
t_en: Please come in, dear guest. We'll have your order ready in no time.

t_ch: 暖黄色灯光下杯盘参差，隔帘透出餐台雾气，木廊尽头飘来木弦琴的余音。
t_en: Under the warm yellow lights, dishes are scattered about, steam wafts through the curtain from the dining table, and the lingering notes of a wooden lute drift from the end of the wooden corridor.

t_ch: 暖黄灯光笼着暗色木质餐桌，炭火气与油脂的香气在空气中交织——饱腹感，那是幸福的另一个名字，你体会到了不做选择，全部都要的快乐。
t_en: The warm yellow light envelops the dark wooden dining table, as the aroma of charcoal and savory oils intertwine in the air. Satiety, that is but another name for happiness. Here, you have discovered the joy of never having to choose, for you can have it all.

t_ch: …好吃。
t_en: ...Delicious.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临。
t_en: Come back next time.

t_ch: 有没有能带走的食物呢？
t_en: Is there any food we can take with us?

t_ch: 小店主打堂食，不做外卖。希望客人能够理解！
t_en: We specialize in dine-in service only, no takeout. We appreciate your understanding!

t_ch: 我有别的事，先离开了。
t_en: I got to go now.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临。
t_en: Come back next time.

t_ch: 你不清楚淡蓝色隔帘后隐藏着什么，就像你不知道为何这个房间会出现在酒馆里。
t_en: You don't know what lies behind the pale blue curtain, just as you don't know why this room is even in the tavern to begin with.

t_ch: 但无论如何，现在，你要把床帘拉开了——
t_en: But regardless, the time has come to open the curtain—

t_ch: 不要拉开！不要拉开！不要拉开！
t_en: Don't open it! Don't do it! Don't!

t_ch: 回应声来自隔帘后的空间，可毫无疑问，透过眼前这层薄纱，你并没有见到任何人影。
t_en: The voice came from behind the curtain. But looking through the thin layer of fabric, you can see no one there.

t_ch: …你是？
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: 为什么不能拉开隔帘？
t_en: Why can't I open the curtain?

t_ch: 这样…有害健康…是的，没有错。不，她一直不省人事。这样啊……
t_en: It's... bad for your health... Yes, that's right. No, she has been unconscious. I see...

t_ch: 对方没有回答，反而似乎正在与不知何处的某人通话。
t_en: The voice doesn't answer you, but instead seems to be talking with someone you can't see.

t_ch: 喂喂！请问是医生吗？是…是的，她一直没有醒来。是的…是的……
t_en: Hello? Is this the doctor? Yes... Yes, she still hasn't woken up. Yes... yes...

t_ch: 她一直在沉睡着。拜托你…叫醒她……
t_en: She's been asleep this whole time. Please... you have to wake her up...

t_ch: 那话语近乎悲鸣，轻声到难以听清的程度。你忽地想起那位医生对花火的诊断，或许，这张病床上躺着被某人遗弃的面具，也或许正好相反。
t_en: The voice was almost a whimper, so quiet you could barely make it out. You suddenly recall the doctor's diagnosis of Sparkle. Maybe there's a mask on this bed, discarded by someone... or maybe it's the other way around.

t_ch: 你还要拉开隔帘吗？当然，你可以交给更*适合*的人来处理。
t_en: Are you still going to open the curtain? Of course, you could always leave this for someone more... capable.

t_ch: 拉开隔帘！
t_en: Open the curtain!

t_ch: 对。正所谓疑心生暗鬼，克服恐惧最好的办法，就是面对恐惧。
t_en: That's right. It's true that your own fear is the real monster. And the best way to conquer a monster is to look it in the eye.

t_ch: 你，无畏恐惧。
t_en: You fear... nothing.

t_ch: 隔帘内没有人。你只见到一只普通的舞鞋，就这么孤零零地摆在病床上。
t_en: There's no one behind the curtain. All you see is a single, ordinary dancing shoe lying on the bed.

t_ch: 难道刚才的对话，就是通过这只舞鞋进行的吗？你始终不得而知。
t_en: Was the conversation you heard coming from this dancing shoe? You'll never know for sure.

t_ch: …还是别打扰了吧。
t_en: It's probably best to leave it alone.

t_ch: 很明显，问题的复杂程度已经远远超过了你的理解能力，贸然介入并没有好处。正当你打算离开时……
t_en: Clearly, this situation is far more complicated than you can comprehend, and there's no point in getting involved. Just as you decide to leave...

t_ch: 喂？请问是医生吗？喂？你有听见吗？…喂？喂？请问有听见吗？你是医生吧？喂？
t_en: Hello? Are you the doctor? Hello? Can you hear me? ...Hello? Is anyone there? You're the doctor, right? Hello?

t_ch: 你好，请问怎么了吗？
t_en: Hello? Is everything alright?

t_ch: …你是医生吗？！那个…我…其实，我一直很想把名字和身份从她手中夺走，因为她看起来好难受。
t_en: Are you the doctor!? The thing is... I... I've always wanted to take her name and identity from her, because she looks so miserable.

t_ch: 但那样做，她会很悲伤的。
t_en: But if I did that, she would be very sad.

t_ch: 那么，你要不要试着和大家一起去上学呢？
t_en: Well then, why don't you try going to school with everyone else?

t_ch: 能接触到各种事，对她也有好处，像是和同学见面，或是听课，我也会一起去的，好吗？
t_en: It would be good for her to experience new things, like meeting new classmates or going to class. I'll be there with you, alright?
...Sure.

t_ch: 此后许久，隔帘内都再没有声音传来。身旁的高中生却不知为何深深叹了口气。
t_en: For a long while after, there was only silence from behind the curtain. The high school student next to you, however, let out a deep sigh for some reason.

t_ch: …你是？
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: 到底是怎么回事？
t_en: What was all that about?

t_ch: 她…有点不一样。对存在感到怀疑，没法正确理解现实，也承受了太多不该有的压力。
t_en: She's... a little different. She questions her own existence and can't properly perceive reality. She's also under far more pressure than she should be.

t_ch: 没关系。我相信她会找到新的生活节奏的。人偶就算摘下面具，内在的心也不会改变。
t_en: It's alright. I'm sure she'll find her own way. Even if a puppet removes its mask, the heart inside remains the same.

t_ch: 再见。谢谢你陪了她这么久。
t_en: Goodbye. And thank you for staying with her all this time.

t_ch: 极其普通的舞鞋，看不出有任何特别。但或许，它也曾是某个女孩年幼时的珍贵之物。
t_en: A perfectly ordinary dancing shoe, with nothing special about it. Still, perhaps it was once a young girl's most prized possession.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你听见了悉悉索索的声响，就从隔帘内部传来。你确定那里有什么东西。
t_en: Undoubtedly, you hear rustling sounds coming from behind the curtain. You're certain something's in there.

t_ch: 拉开看看吧！你的好奇心正在低语，而对此，你没有任何反对意见。于是你伸出了手——
t_en: Pull it open and take a look! Your curiosity is whispering to you, and you have no objections to that. So you reach out your hand...

t_ch: 意料之中的，病床上空空如也，但很显然，你并不会看漏床底下的东西。
t_en: As expected, the hospital bed is empty, but obviously, you won't miss what's under it.

t_ch: …还活着吗？
t_en: ...Are you still alive?

t_ch: 你应该在房里，不该在床底。
t_en: You should be in your room, not under the bed.

t_ch: 床底的噗噜并未对你的话语作出反应，或许，他想把「你没看见我」这个状态坚持到底。
t_en: The ploo beneath the bed doesn't react to your words. Perhaps he's committed to maintaining his "you can't see me" state until the very end.

t_ch: 再不说话我就喊人了！
t_en: Speak up or I'm calling for help!

t_ch: 喂…我是真看见你了。
t_en: Hey... I really can see you.
Shh!

t_ch: 别说话，我正在冥想。我在寻找失去的那一半灵魂，我知道它就在这间病房里。
t_en: Shh, I'm meditating. I'm searching for the lost half of my soul. I know it's somewhere in this ward.

t_ch: 好吧。我失败了。趁下轮尝试还没开始，有什么想问的快说吧。
t_en: Fine. I failed. If you've got any questions, spit them out before I start my next attempt.

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 明知故问。躺在病床上…不对，病床下的还能是谁，当然是需要治疗的病人。
t_en: You already know the answer. Who else could be lying in... no, under... the hospital bed other than a patient who needs treatment?

t_ch: 还有更多信息。这里是「世界尽头」酒馆，所以我是个愚者，在寻找我的面具，这样够清晰了吗？
t_en: There's more information. This is "World's End" Tavern, and I'm a Fool searching for my mask. Is that clear enough?

t_ch: 你在床底下做什么？
t_en: What are you doing under the bed?

t_ch: 哈。这个说来话长了。我来找医生看病，诊断结果是「自我迷失」，治疗方案则是……
t_en: Hah. That's quite a story. I went to see a doctor, and they diagnosed me with "loss of self." The treatment plan was...

t_ch: 广泛的聆听。所以我躲在床底下，窃听其余病人的诊断过程，希望藉此来找到我原本的面具。
t_en: Extensive eavesdropping. So I've been hidden under the bed, secretly listening to other patients' diagnoses, hoping to find my original mask through this.

t_ch: 可我发现，听到的秘密越多，窥探的隐私越多，我…好像离原本的我越来越远了。
t_en: But I've realized... the more secrets I hear, the more private matters I pry into, the further I seem to drift away from who I used to be.

t_ch: 我看见茫茫多窗户，每扇后面都亮着灯，房间里都住着人，但其中唯独没有我自己。我在哪？
t_en: I see countless windows, lights glowing behind each one, people living in every room, but I'm not in any of them. Where am I?

t_ch: 你这是侵犯隐私权！
t_en: That's a violation of privacy!

t_ch: 在酒馆里谈论隐私权，搞笑？那儿的马桶里塞的可全是隐私，想掏尽管去掏，没人拦着。
t_en: Privacy rights in a tavern? That's hilarious! Even the toilets here are stuffed with secrets. Feel free to dig through them if you want, nobody's stopping you.

t_ch: 好笑的、可笑的、可怜的、可爱的…如果不是藏在你最心底的东西，那就没意思了。
t_en: Funny, ridiculous, pitiful, adorable... if it's not hidden in the deepest part of your heart, then what's the point?

t_ch: 这是个庸医吧？
t_en: That doctor has to be a fraud, right?

t_ch: 你知道吗，我就是这个医生。我给我自己看病。
t_en: You know what? I am that doctor. I treat myself.

t_ch: 或许你在房间里的床底下。
t_en: Maybe you're under the bed in your room.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈！好！你讲了个我喜欢的笑话，是啊，躲在床底，看到的当然是床底的世界！
t_en: Hahahahaha! Nice! Now that's my kind of joke. Of course the underside of the bed becomes your world when you're hiding beneath it!

t_ch: 茅塞顿开！一语点醒床底人！想在房间里找到自己，首先我就得站在房间里才行啊！
t_en: That makes sense! Quite enlightening! If I want to find myself in the room, I need to actually stand in the room first!

t_ch: 这个疗程就到此为止。接下来，我要堂堂正正地和来这的病人聊天，聆听他们的话语。
t_en: This course of treatment ends here. From now on, I'll have proper conversations with the patients who come here and listen to what they have to say.

t_ch: 别找了，那是别人的房间。
t_en: Stop looking. That's not your room.

t_ch: 是啊，我知道。但我不是…到处都找不到，才只好用这个办法。说不定能找到很像的呢。
t_en: Yeah, I know. But I can't... find it anywhere else, so this is my only option. Who knows, maybe I'll find something similar.

t_ch: 哎，行吧。这个疗程就到此为止，我该去试试新的方案了。
t_en: *sigh* Alright then. That's enough of this course of treatment. Time to try a new approach.

t_ch: 你也该走了。再见再见，我看得出来，你还不适合这个地方。但酒馆的病房永远欢迎你。
t_en: You should go now. Bye-bye, I can tell this place doesn't suit you well. But you're always welcome in the ward of the tavern.

t_ch: 有什么推荐？
t_en: Any recommendations?

t_ch: 先声明啊！我们禁止任何未成年人饮酒，你要是还没长大，你先长大好吧……
t_en: Disclaimer! We strictly prohibit minors from drinking alcohol. If you're not grown up yet, come back when you are...

t_ch: 你放心，我成年了。
t_en: Don't worry, I'm of legal age.

t_ch: 那没事了，我就是按照规定问问。
t_en: Alright then, just had to ask, protocol and all.

t_ch: 糟糕了，我未成年。
t_en: Oh cripes, I'm underage.

t_ch: 出酒馆左转，有自动贩卖机，那里面有各种各样甜甜的汽水。
t_en: Turn left after exiting the tavern, there's a vending machine with all kinds of sweet sodas inside.

t_ch: 另外，咱这是主理人酒馆，客人来喝什么饮料，得听我这主理人的建议。
t_en: Besides, this is my tavern, and when it comes to what drinks my customers should have, they ought to listen to my recommendations as the proprietor.

t_ch: 看在我们的交情上，我会请你一杯，但你要回答我的问题，我帮你挑，我保证是最懂你的酒保——
t_en: For old times' sake, I'll buy you a drink, but you'll have to answer my questions. I'll pick for you. I promise I'm the drinksmith who knows you best.

t_ch: 第一问，您来咱「世界尽头」酒馆，是为了找点乐子，还是寻找刺激，或者是…我想想，单纯想喝个饮料？
t_en: First question: What brings you to "World's End" Tavern? Are you here for some funsies, looking for a thrill, or... let me think... just want to grab a drink?

t_ch: 来愚者的酒馆就是找乐子的。
t_en: Coming to the Fool's tavern is all about having fun!

t_ch: 哦？找乐子的话，你更喜欢哪种乐子呢？假装喝酒，让嘴巴短路，还是说想来点文艺范儿？
t_en: Oh? Speaking of fun, what's your poison? Pretending to drink till your mouth short-circuits, or are you in the mood for something more... cultured?

t_ch: 假装在喝酒，实际是汽水儿。
t_en: Pretending to drink alcohol, but it's actually just soda.

t_ch: 那我推荐这杯「面包汽水」。
t_en: Then I recommend this "Bread Soda".

t_ch: 看着、闻着、喝着都像是酒，但实际不是酒，在愚者的酒馆里，倒也正常。
t_en: Looks like booze, smells like booze, tastes like booze, but it's not actually booze. Then again, that's pretty normal for the Fool's tavern.

t_ch: 来吧，恭喜您，成功用成年人的杯子，装了一杯未成年人的快乐。
t_en: Well, well, congratulations! You've successfully put a kid's joy in a grown-up's cup.

t_ch: 嘴巴里噼里啪啦的，来点儿。
t_en: Pop, crackle and snap in your mouth, give me some.

t_ch: 那我推荐这杯「噼咔白葡萄汽水」。
t_en: Then I recommend this "Pika White Grape Soda."

t_ch: 含在嘴里保证噼里啪啦的，您看见杯里的弹簧了吗，那是上一位智械客人短路时留下的。
t_en: Keep it in your mouth for a guaranteed pop and crackle! See that spring in the cup? That's from our last Intellitron customer who had a short circuit.

t_ch: 来吧，您也小心点，喝的时候会「噼咔噼咔」的，您别朝着别人吐泡泡就成。
t_en: Here you go, but be careful, because it'll go all "fizz-crackle" when you drink it. Just try not to blow bubbles at other people, alright?

t_ch: 电影里那些人喝啥我也喝啥…
t_en: I'll have whatever they're having in the movies...

t_ch: 你放心，我们这也满足那些刻板印象——来一杯「热夜之都」吧。
t_en: Don't worry, we've got all those stereotypes covered. How about a "Capital of Passion"?

t_ch: 喝了这杯「热夜之都」，就算你只是在这里发呆，看上去都会非常有破碎感——
t_en: After a glass of "Capital of Passion," even if you're just spacing out here, you'll look incredibly delicate and weary...

t_ch: 「有时如果你喜欢一个人，你就要把他当作陌生人。」
t_en: "Sometimes if you love someone, you have to treat them like a stranger."

t_ch: 痛，太痛了…想要这么痛，就得喝「热夜之都」。
t_en: Ouch, it hurts so bad... If you wanna feel this much pain, try drinking "Capital of Passion".

t_ch: 要是寻找刺激，能有多刺激？
t_en: How thrilling could looking for thrills really be?

t_ch: 寻找刺激，那么…你能承受什么程度的刺激？
t_en: Looking for thrills, huh... just how much excitement can you handle?

t_ch: 刺激肠胃的，会上吐下泻那种。
t_en: The kind that messes with your gut and makes you spew from both ends.

t_ch: 那我直接推荐你「催吐剂」吧。
t_en: Then I'll just recommend you the "Vomit Inducing Agent."

t_ch: 虽然它不在菜单上，但您既然诚心诚意地问了…我也大发慈悲地推荐给您。
t_en: Although it's not on the menu, since you asked so sincerely... I'll graciously recommend it to you.

t_ch: 从广义上来看，它里面装着液体，喝了也不会死，姑且也可以算是一种饮品。
t_en: Broadly speaking, it contains liquid and drinking it won't kill you, so I guess you could technically call it a beverage.

t_ch: 喝完记得写一下感想，我会拿去做参考的。
t_en: After you drink it, don't forget to write down your thoughts. I'll use them as reference.

t_ch: 到作死边缘，但不想真的死掉。
t_en: Living on the edge, but not ready to kick the bucket.

t_ch: 有人曾经给了我一个配方，叫「塔拉毒火焰」，大概能达到你想要的效果。
t_en: Someone once gave me a recipe called "Thalan Toxi-Flame," it should do exactly what you're looking for.

t_ch: 据说啊据说…反正我没有喝过。喝下去的感觉就像是生吞点燃的钢丝绒，非常惨痛。
t_en: They say that—I mean, I've never tried it myself, but they say that drinking it feels like swallowing ignited steel wool... absolutely excruciating.

t_ch: 您要是有幸活下来，肯定也是洪福齐天了。
t_en: If you're lucky enough to survive, you must be blessed by the heavens.

t_ch: 有没有那种像是被子弹打中的？
t_en: Did it feel like getting hit by a bullet?

t_ch: 有的，之前有位牛仔来这里给我们推荐了一个配方：「阿斯德纳白橡木」。
t_en: Yeah, a cowboy came by earlier and recommended us a recipe: "Asdana's White Oak".

t_ch: 尝一口，感觉跟脑袋上中了一枪也没差别了——颅骨嗡鸣，记忆短路，很有可能会流鼻血。
t_en: One bite, and it feels like getting shot in the head. Your skull buzzing, memory short-circuiting, and you'll probably end up with a nosebleed.

t_ch: 另外，小馋嘴要小心一点，别把杯子里的「配菜」也给吞下去了。
t_en: Also, you little glutton, be careful not to swallow the "garnish" in your cup too.

t_ch: 喝完之后能够登神的那种有吗？
t_en: Got anything that'll make me ascend to godhood after drinking it?

t_ch: 我不确定这是否算得上刺激，但是你可以试试：「仙馔蜜酒（仿品）」。
t_en: I'm not sure if this counts as exciting, but you could try: "Ambrosia (Imitation)."

t_ch: 传说，第一个尝到仙馔蜜酒的凡人成为了神明。在咽下的瞬间，他看见世界的规则变成了手边的丝线，他想怎么扯就怎么扯，想多拽就多拽……
t_en: Legend has it that the first mortal to taste the divine nectar became a God. In the moment they swallowed, they saw the rules of the world transform into threads at their fingertips, ready to be pulled and tugged at their whim...

t_ch: 可惜，我这只有仿品，您喝下去之后也许没有那么刺激。
t_en: Too bad I only have replicas here. Might not give you quite the same kick when you drink it.

t_ch: 我就喝个饮料而已，简单点…
t_en: I'm just getting a drink, keep it simple...

t_ch: 嗐，别把我当成一个到处取乐的乐子人嘛。没关系，满足你，你对味道有什么要求吗？
t_en: Hey, don't treat me like some fun lovin' goofball who's just here for laughs. No worries, I'll help you out. Got any specific preferences for flavor?

t_ch: 有味道就行，别太怪了。
t_en: As long as it has flavor, just don't make it too weird.

t_ch: 拿着吧，矿泉水。
t_en: Here, take this mineral water.

t_ch: 您的选择非常安全，安全到…「世界尽头」酒馆也不得不拿出最后的手段。
t_en: Your choice is so safe, so safe that... even the "World's End" Tavern has to resort to their last measure.

t_ch: 除非您的思维中存在着某种思想钢印，咱们的矿泉水应该是绝对不会出错的选择！
t_en: Unless you've got some kind of mental seal in your consciousness, our mineral water should be an absolutely foolproof choice!

t_ch: …有没有那种传统点的？
t_en: ...Don't you have anything more traditional?

t_ch: 还真有，这是我上次仙舟旅行带回来的「琥珀花雕」。
t_en: Yes indeed, this is the Amber Huadiao Wine I brought back from my last trip to Xianzhou.

t_ch: 虽然在时髦的酒馆点一杯黄酒不太传统，但我向您保证这一口文化底蕴绝对是传统的。
t_en: While ordering yellow wine at a trendy tavern might not be a tradition, I assure you that this sip of cultural heritage is as traditional as it gets.

t_ch: 如果你需要，我这里还有一碟茴香豆，可以搭配着使用。
t_en: If you need it, I've got a plate of fennel seeds here that you can use as well.

t_ch: …有没有那种保守点的？
t_en: ...Got anything a bit more modest?

t_ch: 恐怕没有什么比「在酒馆点一杯羊奶」更保守的选择了，这是「热浮羊奶」。
t_en: Nothing's more vanilla than "ordering a glass of goat milk at a tavern." This is steamed puffergoat milk.

t_ch: 虽然您不是个牛仔，但在酒馆点了羊奶。为什么不点杯烈酒，因为烈酒伤身体。
t_en: Though you're no cowboy, you ordered goat milk at the tavern. Why not get some hard liquor? Because hard liquor's bad for your health.

t_ch: 在酒馆的声色犬马之中，您毅然决然坚定地选择养生，您…您是这个！
t_en: Amidst all the debauchery at the tavern, you resolutely chose to stay healthy... you... you're absolutely mad!

t_ch: 钟珊珊说不定真是…最不懂你的酒保呢！管他的，先干为敬！
t_en: Zhongshanshan might just be... the drinksmith who understands you the least! But who cares? Let's drink to that!

t_ch: 牙齿比出生的时候还干净，呼吸都变清爽了，体会到刷牙的精髓。
t_en: My teeth are cleaner than when I was born, and even my breath feels much fresher now. I finally get what brushing teeth is all about!

t_ch: 就像换了一口新牙，原来牙齿可以这么完美！笑容也变更自信了。
t_en: It's like getting a brand new set of teeth! I never knew they could be this perfect! My smile's got so much more confidence now.

t_ch: 甜度恰到好处，幸福得想眯起眼睛叹气…所有不开心都被赶跑了。
t_en: The sweetness is just perfect, making me want to sigh contentedly with half-closed eyes... All the unhappiness has been chased away.

t_ch: 到底是多久没有在吃饭的时候又唱又跳了呢…肚子和心都填满了。
t_en: It's been forever since I last sang and danced while eating... Both my stomach and heart are full.

t_ch: 一杯极致的幽默在嘴里化开，变成了笑声。体会到一丝欢愉。
t_en: A sip of exquisite humor dissolves in my mouth, turning into laughter. I feel a touch of Elation.

t_ch: 普通的病床、普通的帘子…至少在此刻看来，它们没有任何特别之处。
t_en: Normal hospital beds, normal curtains... At least for now, there's nothing special about them whatsoever.

t_ch: 拉动帘子或许能为你带来快乐，但现在不是做这种事的时候。
t_en: Pulling on the curtain might bring you joy, but now's not the time for that.

t_ch: 你注意到隔帘边缘有不少磨损，或许，也有许多像你这样的人曾专程前来反复拉动过。
t_en: You notice the curtain is frayed at the edges. Perhaps many like you have made the trip here just to pull it back and forth over and over.

t_ch: 即便是在酒馆，也还是请尊重下人们的隐私权吧…至少你心中的崇高道德感是如此认为的。
t_en: Even in the tavern, a little respect for privacy ought to be observed... or at least that's what your lofty sense of morality tells you.

t_ch: 俗话说，愈是朦胧神秘的东西，愈能激起人们的探索欲望。而眼前薄薄的蓝色纱帘，无疑正是这个理论的最佳阐释。
t_en: As the saying goes, the more mysterious and enigmatic something is, the more it sparks our desire to explore it. And these thin, sheer blue curtains before you are undoubtedly the perfect embodiment of that expression.

t_ch: 新的动静出现了…怎么办，你似乎有点按捺不住自己的好奇心了，要掀开看看吗？
t_en: There's a new sound... What should you do? You seem unable to contain your curiosity... Would you like to lift the curtains and take a peek?

t_ch: 先听听里面的动静。
t_en: First, listen carefully to the sounds inside.

t_ch: 遇事不决，谨慎点总归是没错的。你屏息凝神，开始认真聆听起隔帘内的声响——
t_en: When in doubt, it's always better to err on the side of caution. Holding your breath, you focus intently, carefully listening to the sounds coming from behind the curtains...

t_ch: 漫长的寂静中，一阵微不可闻的叹息声幽幽地从其中传来。
t_en: In the long silence, a barely audible sigh drifted faintly through the air.

t_ch: 又是…陌生的天花板。
t_en: Again... an unfamiliar ceiling.

t_ch: 尝试与对方聊天。
t_en: Try to chat with them.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你是个有礼貌的人，既然对方都向你搭话了，你当然没有不回应的道理。
t_en: Without a doubt, you're a polite person. Since they've taken the initiative to talk to you, there's no reason not to respond.

t_ch: 开场白很重要，它决定了你留给对方的第一印象，在这种关键时候，你打算说的第一句话是——
t_en: Opening lines are crucial. They determine the first impression you leave on the other person. At this crucial moment, your first words will be...

t_ch: 你好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 再见了，所有的医务室。
t_en: Farewell, to all infirmaries.

t_ch: 真恶心…
t_en: That's disgusting...

t_ch: 不能逃避，不能逃避…
t_en: Mustn't run away, mustn't run away...

t_ch: 好，你极为热情地向对方打了招呼，并好奇会得到什么样的回应，这时…隔帘突然自动拉开了！
t_en: Alright, you've greeted them with extreme enthusiasm. Just as you wonder what kind of response you'll get... The curtain slides open on its own!

t_ch: 果断点，直接拉开隔帘！
t_en: Just go for it, pull back that curtain!

t_ch: 想那么多干嘛，最坏的结果无非就是低头认个错，笑着将眼前的尴尬场景化解掉。在你那即将喷涌而出的开拓精神面前，这算不了什么，对吧？
t_en: Why overthink it? The worst that could happen is having to bow your head and apologize, then laugh it off to diffuse the awkward situation. It's nothing compared to that Trailblazing spirit about to burst forth from within you, right?

t_ch: 所以现在，你要行动了。
t_en: So now, it's time for you to take action.

t_ch: …你好好休息，打扰了。
t_en: ...Get some rest. Sorry to bother you.

t_ch: 是啊，医务室原本便是为健康状况不佳的学生准备的，在这胡乱探索，势必会影响对方的恢复。
t_en: Yeah, the infirmary is meant for students who aren't feeling well. Snooping around this place would definitely interfere with their recovery.

t_ch: 这种时候，只用默默离开就好了。
t_en: At times like these, it's best to just quietly slip away.

t_ch: …你好好休息，打扰了。
t_en: ...Get some rest. Sorry to bother you.

t_ch: 是啊，医务室原本便是为健康状况不佳的学生准备的，在这胡乱探索，势必会影响对方的恢复。
t_en: Yeah, the infirmary is meant for students who aren't feeling well. Snooping around this place would definitely interfere with their recovery.

t_ch: 这种时候，只用默默离开就好了。
t_en: At times like these, it's best to just quietly slip away.

t_ch: 你好。请问…这是哪，我已经不用再战斗了？
t_en: Hello. Um... Where am I? I don't have to fight anymore?

t_ch: 你来得太晚了。
t_en: You are too late.

t_ch: 这样吗？原来冲击已经结束了，我究竟…昏迷了多久。十四年…还是更长？
t_en: Is that so? So the impact is over... How long was I out? Fourteen years... or even longer?

t_ch: 还没到结束的时候！
t_en: It's not over yet!

t_ch: 果然，由我引起的灾难…我的罪过，不会轻易消失。
t_en: Sure enough... The disaster I caused... My sins won't fade away easily.

t_ch: 谢谢你的照顾。但我…我必须去找我的朋友们了。她们或许还在月亮上，等着我去拯救。
t_en: Thank you for taking care of me. But I... I must find my friends. They might still be on the moon, waiting for me to save them.

t_ch: 那轮幻月，你应该看到了。那是来自天外的不明生命体，我的使命，就是消灭它。
t_en: That Phantasmoon, you must have seen it. It's an unknown lifeform from beyond the sky, and my mission is to eliminate it.

t_ch: 我必须坐上机铠，那是只有我能做到的事。咳…再见，陌生人。
t_en: I must pilot the mechatron, that's something only I can do. *cough* ...Farewell, stranger.

t_ch: 普通的医务室床位。尽管曾有人躺在这里，但如今床单和被褥早已整理得没有一丝褶皱。
t_en: A standard infirmary bed. Though someone once lay here, the sheets and bedding have since been neatly made without a single wrinkle.

t_ch: 祝他的旅途顺利——你在心底如此祈愿。
t_en: In your heart, you pray that they have a smooth journey.

t_ch: 随处可见的医务室床帘，深沉的蓝色透出一丝神秘。如今它紧紧地闭着，似乎在隐藏某个秘密。
t_en: This is the kind of infirmary curtain you can find anywhere. Colored deep blue, it exudes an air of mystery. Now it's drawn tightly shut, as if concealing some secret.

t_ch: 掀开看看吧！你仿佛听到了恶魔的低语。掀开看看吧！你不由自主地向隔帘伸出了手……
t_en: Take a peek! You seem to hear the devil's whispering. Take a peek! Your hand involuntarily reaches for the curtain...

t_ch: 哟，{F#少女}{M#少年}，你也是来寻找记忆的吗？
t_en: Yo, {F#girl}{M#boy}, are you also here to search for memories?

t_ch: 突如其来的话语，嗓音嘶哑而慵懒，像是从繁星遍布的暗夜传来。
t_en: The words are spoken by a hoarse and languid voice, as if carried forth from a star-studded night.

t_ch: 别紧张。这里现在只有我们，直到满足今天的自己为止，试着来聊聊天吧。
t_en: Don't be nervous. It's just us here now, so let's have a chat until we're satisfied with ourselves goes.

t_ch: …你是谁？
t_en: ...Who are you?

t_ch: …我？城市暗处的游荡者，夜晚的主宰，以人类生命为食料的怪物。怎么称呼，随你喜欢都好。
t_en: ...Me? A wanderer in the dark corners of the city, ruler of the night, a monster that feeds on human lives. Call me whatever you like.

t_ch: 或者你可以用个更直接的名字——吸血鬼。
t_en: Or you could just use a more straightforward name: vampire.

t_ch: 哈。现在明白了吗？
t_en: Hah. Get it now?

t_ch: …你从哪里来？
t_en: ...Where are you from?

t_ch: 有意思。从哪来很重要吗？有谁说得清楚？我们在这里相遇，这件事才是最重要的吧？
t_en: Interesting. Does it really matter where we're from? Who can say for sure? What matters most is that we met here, right?

t_ch: 不过，我来这里，确实是为了寻找我的过去。人类都是在医院出生的对吧，或许我也不例外。
t_en: Still, it's true that I came here to search for my past. Humans are all born in hospitals, right? Maybe I'm no exception.

t_ch: 啊，不准说这里不是医院！我知道的，但这不是，实在找不到了，就来碰碰运气嘛。
t_en: Nuh-uh, you're not allowed to tell me this isn't a hospital! I know it's not, but I couldn't find one anywhere else, so I thought I'd try my luck here.

t_ch: …你要到哪去？
t_en: ...Where are you going?

t_ch: 喂，你就不觉得这些问题很无趣嘛？就算得到了结果，又能有什么改变？
t_en: Hey, don't you think these questions are boring? Even if we get the answers, what's gonna change anyway?

t_ch: 行吧，我就是随便转转，运气好，能找到我要的答案。运气不好，也能带几袋暗红色的能量饮料回去。
t_en: Fine, I was just wandering around. If I'm lucky, I'll find the answers I'm looking for. If not, at least I can bring back a few bags of crimson Energy Drinks.

t_ch: 至于去哪…实话说，我没想好。去哪都行，总之，能看到那个月亮就好吧。
t_en: As for where to go... Honestly, I haven't decided yet. Anywhere is fine, as long as I can see that moon.

t_ch: …你的话可以相信吗？
t_en: ...Can I trust what you're saying?

t_ch: 我说，你到底有完没完啊！
t_en: Come on, when are you gonna give it a rest already!

t_ch: 这里是医务室，禁止大声喧哗！问来问去的，烦死了。
t_en: This is the infirmary, keep your voice down! All these questions are driving me nuts.

t_ch: 我就是来找东西的。出生证明也好，旧照片也好，能表明我是怎么出现在这个世界上的东西都行。但什么都找不到，这太奇怪了吧？
t_en: I'm just here looking for stuff. Birth certificate, old photos, anything that could show how I came into this world. But I can't find a single thing. Isn't that super weird?

t_ch: 喂。你看起来很懂的样子，知道些什么吗？
t_en: Hey. You seem to know your stuff, got any insights to share?

t_ch: …为什么吸血鬼会长兔耳朵啊！
t_en: ...Why would vampires grow bunny ears!?

t_ch: 奇怪？电视屏幕里，这种角色不是很多嘛？还是说…嘿，你不喜欢？
t_en: Huh? Aren't there tons of characters like that on TV? Or wait... Hey, don't tell me you don't like them?

t_ch: 无所谓吧，和人类长得不一样就行，至于是兔耳朵还是獠牙，不重要。
t_en: Whatever, as long as I don't look like a human. Whether I have bunny ears or fangs, it doesn't matter.

t_ch: 或许你是幻造种。
t_en: Maybe you're an imagenae.

t_ch: 唔，我好像听过这个称呼，但是啊，这不过是用一个词解释另一个词，最终还是什么都没搞清楚，没意思。
t_en: Hmm, I think I've heard that term before. But hey, that's just semantics. In the end, we still haven't figured anything out. What's the point?

t_ch: 我不记得过去的事，所以怎么说都行。重要的是，我能不能认可。
t_en: I don't remember my past, so you can say whatever you want. What matters is whether I can accept it or not.

t_ch: 或许你是外星人。
t_en: Maybe you're an alien.

t_ch: 噢！有意思，怪不得我对那个月亮很有好感，原来，我是从那来的啊！
t_en: Oh! That's interesting, no wonder I felt drawn to that moon. So that's where I'm from!

t_ch: 我该回那去吗？又该怎么回去呢…啊啊，好复杂，想不清楚。
t_en: Should I go back there? And how would I even get back... Ugh, it's so complicated, I can't think straight.

t_ch: 算啦。既然在这没收获，不如回家喝几瓶胡萝卜汁，再睡大觉！哈哈。
t_en: Oh, well. Since there's nothing here for me, I might as well head home, chug some carrot juice, and hit the hay! Haha.

t_ch: 恭喜你认识了一名忘不掉的神秘美少女，现在我要走咯！
t_en: Congrats on meeting an unforgettable mysterious beauty! Now I gotta run!

t_ch: 有缘分再见吧，{F#少女}{M#少年}。
t_en: If fate allows, we'll meet again. Until next time, {F#girl}{M#boy}.

t_ch: 还是赶紧离开吧。
t_en: Let's get out of here quick.

t_ch: 消毒水味道的病房，紧闭的隔帘，从不知何处传来的话语…所有要素都隐隐透出令人恐惧的气息。
t_en: A hospital room reeking of disinfectant, tightly drawn curtains, mysterious voices coming from who knows where... Every element here exudes an unsettling, fear-inducing vibe.

t_ch: 趁你还没有踏入危险的漩涡，收手吧！
t_en: Back off while you still can, before you get sucked into this dangerous whirlpool!

t_ch: 还是赶紧离开吧。
t_en: Let's get out of here quick.

t_ch: 消毒水味道的病房，紧闭的隔帘，从不知何处传来的话语…所有要素都隐隐透出令人恐惧的气息。
t_en: A hospital room reeking of disinfectant, tightly drawn curtains, mysterious voices coming from who knows where... Every element here exudes an unsettling, fear-inducing vibe.

t_ch: 趁你还没有踏入危险的漩涡，收手吧！
t_en: Back off while you still can, before you get sucked into this dangerous whirlpool!

t_ch: 还是赶紧离开吧。
t_en: Let's get out of here quick.

t_ch: 消毒水味道的病房，紧闭的隔帘，从不知何处传来的话语…所有要素都隐隐透出令人恐惧的气息。
t_en: A hospital room reeking of disinfectant, tightly drawn curtains, mysterious voices coming from who knows where... Every element here exudes an unsettling, fear-inducing vibe.

t_ch: 趁你还没有踏入危险的漩涡，收手吧！
t_en: Back off while you still can, before you get sucked into this dangerous whirlpool!

t_ch: 还是赶紧离开吧。
t_en: Let's get out of here quick.

t_ch: 消毒水味道的病房，紧闭的隔帘，从不知何处传来的话语…所有要素都隐隐透出令人恐惧的气息。
t_en: A hospital room reeking of disinfectant, tightly drawn curtains, mysterious voices coming from who knows where... Every element here exudes an unsettling, fear-inducing vibe.

t_ch: 趁你还没有踏入危险的漩涡，收手吧！
t_en: Back off while you still can, before you get sucked into this dangerous whirlpool!

t_ch: 别打开了，万一另一个自己还没有顺利回到「门」的后面，这样的后果可承受不起。
t_en: Don't open it. We can't risk the consequences if my other self hasn't made it safely behind the "Door" yet.

t_ch: 终有一天你会明白另一个自己从何而来…或许，你已经明白了。
t_en: One day, you'll understand where your other self came from... Or perhaps, you already do.

t_ch: 直接拉开隔帘！
t_en: Just pull the curtains open!

t_ch: 医务室的设施都有它最合适的使用方式，显然，站在床上并不寻常，这意味你必须伸出援手。
t_en: The infirmary's facilities all have their proper uses, and clearly, standing on the bed isn't normal, which means you need to lend a helping hand.

t_ch: 转身离开这里。
t_en: Turn around and leave this place.

t_ch: …等等，这一幕，我似曾相识。
t_en: ...Wait, I've seen this before.

t_ch: 我好像也有点混乱。
t_en: I'm kinda confused too.

t_ch: 你听我说，事情不是你想的那样。
t_en: Listen to me, it's not what you think it is.

t_ch: 应该…还是如我想的那样。
t_en: It should be... just as I thought.

t_ch: 是吗？看来你已经搞清楚事情来龙去脉了。
t_en: Oh, really? Looks like you've figured out what's going on here.

t_ch: 到底是哪样，我没想过…
t_en: What is what I think this is? I've never really thought about it...

t_ch: 是吗？我还以为我面对如此意外，已经很有经验了。
t_en: Really? And there I was, thinking that I was already quite experienced in dealing with such surprises.

t_ch: 冷静，你听我说——
t_en: Calm down, let me explain...

t_ch: 你就是我，我就是你。没错…吧？
t_en: I am you, and you are me. That's right... right?

t_ch: 你问我，我问谁去啊？
t_en: You're asking me, but who am I supposed to ask?

t_ch: 说到底，我对自己的精神状况也没有那么大的信心。你也很清楚，对吧？
t_en: To be honest, I'm not that confident about my mental state either. You know that, don't you?

t_ch: 结果你还是不太确定？
t_en: So you're still not sure?

t_ch: 对啊，毕竟能看到这么混乱的情景，确定的事情也变得不确定了。
t_en: Yeah, after witnessing such chaos, even the things we were sure about don't seem so certain anymore.

t_ch: 总之，我是推开了一扇门之后来到这里的。在脑子回过味之前，我就打算回去了。
t_en: Long story short, I got here after opening a door. Before my brain could even process what was happening, I was already planning my way back.

t_ch: 无论你是过去、未来、或者是幻造出来的我，拉上帘子，我们重归各自的世界线。
t_en: Whether you're from the past, the future, or an imagenated version of me... Draw the curtains, and let's each return to our own timelines.

t_ch: （拉上帘子）
t_en: (Close the curtains)

t_ch: 谢谢。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: （什么都不做）
t_en: (Do nothing)

t_ch: 看来「我」受到的冲击太大，还是我自己来吧。
t_en: Looks like "I" am too shocked to handle this, so I'll do it myself.

t_ch: 帘子已经合上，里面似乎已经没有别的动静了。
t_en: The curtains have been drawn, and there doesn't seem to be any more movement inside.

t_ch: 绘世学院的医务室纱帘隐约透出些阴影，有人在里面，不是躺着，而是正站在床上！
t_en: Through the curtains of Graphia Academy infirmary, a shadow can be seen. Someone was in there, not lying down, but standing right on the bed!

t_ch: 说不定是个怪人呢……
t_en: Maybe they're some kind of weirdo...

t_ch: 电话亭里的电话铃声响个不停，要去接听一下吗？有种不祥的预感……
t_en: The phone in the phone booth keeps ringing. Should you answer it? You have a bad feeling about this...

t_ch: 接一下，说不定有事找我呢…
t_en: Take it. Perhaps someone's looking for me...

t_ch: 不是我喜欢的电话，我不接。
t_en: I don't like this call, so I won't answer it.

t_ch: 就让电话铃继续响下去吧，城市的喧嚣声里，要是少了它可不行。
t_en: Let the phone keep ringing. The city's symphony just wouldn't be complete without it.

t_ch: 喂？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 喂喂，你好，我是玛丽小姐，你为什么把我丢掉呢？我一定会去找你的。
t_en: Hey, hey! Hello there! This is Miss Mary speaking. Why'd you toss me out like that? I'm definitely gonna come find you!

t_ch: 我不记得我有把你丢掉啊？
t_en: I don't remember throwing you away though?

t_ch: 好狠心的人，不仅将玛丽遗弃在河里，还假装不记得这件事。
t_en: How cruel! Not only did they abandon Mary in the river, they're even pretending they don't remember doing it.

t_ch: 你是玛丽，请问玛丽是谁？
t_en: You are Mary, but who is Mary?

t_ch: 好狠心的人，连自己最喜欢的洋娃娃名字都不记得了。
t_en: How cruel! You don't even remember the name of your favorite doll.

t_ch: 你找错人了，这边挂了哈。
t_en: Wrong number. Hanging up now, bye!

t_ch: 别想走，电话亭的门已经自动焊死了，在我来之前，你哪都别想去。
t_en: Don't even think about leaving. The phone booth door's been automatically welded shut. You're not going anywhere until I get there.

t_ch: 玛丽小姐来帮你回忆一下。
t_en: Let me help you remember.

t_ch: 小时候，我是你最喜欢的洋娃娃。但是你长大了，你狠心将玛丽小姐放在衣橱积灰。
t_en: When you were little, I was your favorite doll. But you grew up, and heartlessly left me in the closet.

t_ch: 然后你搬了家，你没有带走我，反手将玛丽小姐扔到了垃圾桶里，就像是扔掉了一块烂布。
t_en: Then you moved away, leaving me behind, and even tossing me into the trashcan like some worthless rag.

t_ch: 现在，我现在就在垃圾桶里，我一定会去找你的。
t_en: Right now, I'm in the trashcan, and I'm definitely coming to find you.

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话亭已经被上锁了…难道我是真的撞上了二相乐园的都市怪谈了？
t_en: The phone booth is locked... Could I have actually stumbled upon one of Planarcadia's Urban Myths?

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 接电话。
t_en: Answer the call.

t_ch: 不接电话。
t_en: Ignore the call.

t_ch: 电话铃声不响了，但是答录机自动响起了玛丽小姐的声音…等等，电话亭里有这么复古的东西吗？
t_en: The phone stops ringing, but an answering machine kicks in with Miss Mary's voice... Wait, do phone booths even have something that retro anymore?

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在贝洛伯格下层区。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm currently in the Underworld of Belobog.

t_ch: 合着你要从那么老远赶过来啊？
t_en: Wait, so you're traveling all the way here from way over there?
